title: Writing Quality Ontology — Concept Entry
description: Frontmatter schema for concept entries in the Writing Quality Ontology.

x-changelog.0.1.0: Initial 5-layer flat taxonomy (Functional, Computational, Structural, Micro, Stylistic).
x-changelog.0.2.0: Expanded to 6 classification dimensions; added compositional stage, scale, concern type, evaluator, determinism.
x-changelog.0.3.0: Added D7 writing_stage (descriptive) and D8 address_when (triage/prescriptive); added D9 engineering_stage. Layer 4 renamed Mechanical; Layer 5 renamed Editorial.
x-changelog.0.4.0: Added D10 impact, D11 risk_severity, D12 responsible_role. Distinguished evaluator (detects) from responsible_role (owns). Added prerequisite_for and cascade_threshold.
x-changelog.0.5.0: Added D13 audience_sensitivity. Split 'accessibility' impact into accessibility-technical, accessibility-presentation, accessibility-cognitive. Added cognitive-overload and inclusivity to impact enum. Noted that risk_severity is audience-conditional.
x-changelog.0.6.0: Added section-level and corpus-level to D3 scale enum, covering publishing-stage and computational concerns that operate above the document unit (site-wide metadata, corpus embedding quality, system-level SEO).

description.description: One-sentence summary used in search results and link popovers.

tags.description: Quartz tags. Use hierarchical form: layer:mechanical, writing-stage:editing, etc.

also_known_as.description: Informal names, common synonyms, or discipline-specific aliases.

wordnet.description: Pre-pulled WordNet data. Populated by scripts/pull_wordnet.py — do not edit manually.

related_forms.description: Hypernym chain (1 level) and closely related forms.

classification.description: Multi-dimensional classification. All fields optional; populate what is known.

layer.description: D1 — Editorial layer(s). Primary home + cascade target if applicable.

stage.description: D2 — Compositional stage. When in the writing lifecycle does this apply?

scale.description: D3 — Granularity. What unit of text does this concern operate on?

concern_type.description: D4 — What kind of quality is being evaluated?

evaluator.description: D5 — Who or what can detect this concern?

determinism.description: D6 — Rule type. Is this a hard rule (binary) or a probabilistic risk?

writing_stage.description: D7 — Writing process stage(s) where this concern is active or relevant (descriptive, multi-valued).

address_when.description: D8 — Triage: the earliest writing stage at which addressing this concern is not premature. Single value. Do not fix commas before structure is stable.

engineering_stage.description: D9 — Engineering design stage(s) with an analogous concern (descriptive, multi-valued).

impact.description: D10 — Downstream real-world consequences when this concern fails. Multi-valued. Note: cognitive-overload is a mediating impact (mechanism); others are terminal impacts.

risk_severity.description: D11 — Stakes of failure. Note: severity is audience-conditional (see D13); this value reflects the general-reader baseline.

responsible_role.description: D12 — Who owns fixing this concern in a production workflow. Distinct from D5 (evaluator = who detects it).

audience_sensitivity.description: D13 — Reader profiles for whom this concern carries heightened risk relative to the general-reader baseline. Omit if the concern applies uniformly to all readers.

cascade_threshold.description: For concerns with cascade effects: the threshold at which a lower-layer concern (e.g. Mechanical) escalates to a higher-layer failure (e.g. Structural coherence collapse).

relationships.description: Typed edges to other concept nodes. Keys are relationship type names (open-ended — new types may be added as the ontology develops). Values are concept id(s).

x-known-relationship-types.subtype_of: This concept is a specific kind of the target.
x-known-relationship-types.aspect_of: This concept is one facet or measurable property of the target.
x-known-relationship-types.impediment_to: This concept is a deterministic obstruction to the target.
x-known-relationship-types.increases_risk_of: This concept raises the probability of the target (probabilistic, not certain).
x-known-relationship-types.controlled_by: This concept is governed, constrained, or diagnosed by the target.
x-known-relationship-types.prerequisite_for: The target concern cannot be usefully addressed until this concern is resolved first.
x-known-relationship-types.measured_by: The target is a metric or method that quantifies this concept.
x-known-relationship-types.operationalized_as: The target is how this abstract concept is made concrete or detectable.
x-known-relationship-types.commonly_confused_with: The target is frequently mistaken for this concept; they are distinct.
x-known-relationship-types.related_to: Non-directional association; use when no more specific type fits.
x-known-relationship-types.part_of: This concept is a component of the target.
x-known-relationship-types.addresses: This concept is a technique or practice that corrects or mitigates the target.
x-known-relationship-types.produces: Applying or violating this concept generates the target as an output.

diagnostic_question.description: The single most useful question an editor asks to detect a failure of this concern.

evaluation_tools.description: Tools, methods, or procedures that evaluate this concern (human or automated).

layerEnum.description: The five editorial layers. Mechanical = correctness (universal rules); Editorial = appropriateness (conventions + pragmatics).

determinismEnum.description: rule-violation = binary wrong/right. risk-factor = probabilistic. philosophy = quality lens spanning multiple dimensions.

impactEnum.description: cognitive-overload is a mediating impact (the mechanism); reader-comprehension etc. are terminal impacts. accessibility split into three distinct concerns by layer.

riskSeverityEnum.description: Baseline severity for the general reader. See D13 audience_sensitivity for populations where severity is higher.

audienceSensitivityEnum.description: Reader profiles for whom this concern's risk_severity is elevated above the general-reader baseline. 'general' is the implicit default and is not listed here.
